summary:
The levator veli palatini muscle receives exclusive innervation via the vagus nerve while others are part of cranial nerves involved with mastication or facial sensation; hence it fits best for being directly controlled solely through the vagal pathway. \(\blacksquare\) Agent1 concludes:

The levator veli palatini muscle is distinctly innervated only by the vagus nerve compared to other nearby muscles which rely more broadly upon branches of the fifth cranial nerve (trigeminal), thus fulfilling the requirement specified in your query accurately. Hence,

\[ \textbf{\underline{Answer}} : \textit{(A) Levator veli palatini}. \]
